systemHealth.title: System Health
systemHealth.description: Monitor providers, storage, scheduling, publishing readiness, project setup, and operational blockers from one place.
systemHealth.alexReady: Alex is ready. Open Alex to chat.
systemHealth.missingAddEnv: Missing. Add in Vercel Environment Variables.
systemHealth.backupCenterDesc: Create sanitized JSON and Markdown workspace exports. Secrets, provider tokens, raw env values, task execution credentials, and binary asset files are excluded.

security.title: Security Center
security.description: Review authentication, workspace isolation, RLS, file uploads, secrets, tokens, AI safety, scheduler protection, headers, audit logging, and build safety.
security.scoreDescription: This score is based only on checks the app can verify from code, migrations, runtime workspace access, and safe configuration presence. Unknown items are marked Needs Review.
security.checklistDesc: OWASP-inspired defensive checks. Needs Review means the app cannot fully verify the control automatically.
security.groupedChecklist: Grouped checklist for authentication, authorization, RLS, files, secrets, AI, GitHub, scheduler, headers, logging, and build safety.
security.backupSecurityDesc: Backup Center availability and export safety controls.
security.issuesDesc: Findings from static code checks, migration review, runtime workspace checks, and safe configuration checks. Secret values are never shown.
security.owaspDesc: Guidance alignment only. This is not an OWASP certification or penetration test.
security.backupAvailableDesc: Safe workspace exports are available at /dashboard/backups.
security.secretsExcludedDesc: Tokens, API keys, and credentials are excluded from exports.
security.restoreDisabledDesc: This phase performs no restore writes to the database.
security.securityMisconfigurationDesc: Security headers, env presence checks, cron protection.
security.supplyChainDesc: Build checks and dependency audit recommendation.
security.cryptographicFailuresDesc: Token encryption readiness and no secret exposure in UI.
security.injectionDesc: Server action validation and upload allowlists.
security.loggingAlertingDesc: Audit log table migration and publish/scheduler logs.
security.noIssues: No issues detected by current checks.
security.manualReview: Continue manual review before major production changes.
security.authRequired: Authentication and an active workspace are required.
security.internalReviewNotice: This page is an internal defensive review center aligned with selected OWASP Top 10 and ASVS-style verification controls. It does not claim full compliance or certification.

backups.title: Backup Center
backups.description: Create safe workspace backups for projects, prompts, releases, content, settings, and operational data.
backups.safetyMessage: This center exports sanitized workspace data only. It does not restore, overwrite, delete, deploy, run tasks, touch provider publishing, change scheduler logic, export env values, include binary files, or expose tokens/secrets.
backups.backupHistoryDesc: Only metadata and summaries are stored. Old full backup JSON files are not stored in the database.
backups.restorePreviewDesc: Restoring backups is intentionally disabled in this phase.
backups.noBackupsCreatedDesc: Create a backup to generate an immediate JSON download and save a metadata-only history row.
backups.restoreNotEnabled: Restoring backups is not enabled yet to avoid accidental overwrites. This Backup Center currently supports safe export and reference backups only.

safePatchPlanner.title: Safe Patch Planner
safePatchPlanner.description: Plan code changes safely before implementation. Review affected files, risks, tests, and rollback steps before sending prompts to Codex.
safePatchPlanner.planningMessage: This planner does not modify repository files, write to GitHub, create pull requests, deploy, run task execution, publish provider content, or expose secrets.
safePatchPlanner.howToUsePrompt: How to use the generated prompt
safePatchPlanner.howToUseMessage: Copy the Codex implementation prompt only after the approval checklist is reviewed. Keep the implementation scope narrow, verify with lint/typecheck/build, and keep the no-touch systems intact.

softwarePlanner.title: Software Planner
softwarePlanner.description: Turn a software idea into an architecture plan, MVP scope, route map, schema draft, testing checklist, deployment checklist, and pending task drafts.
softwarePlanner.planningMessage: The planner does not generate repository files, push commits, create pull requests, deploy, publish provider content, or run task execution automatically.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Read system alerts, task updates, provider setup messages, scheduler results, and publishing activity.

reports.title: Reports
reports.description: Track publishing readiness, content performance, provider status, and workflow activity.
reports.healthSummaryDesc: Operational health score and top next actions from the System Health Center.
reports.publishingStatusDesc: Visual summary of real Content Studio item states.
reports.providerReadinessDesc: Current provider status and next safe action. No credentials are displayed.
reports.schedulerSummaryDesc: Execution status from scheduled Content Studio records.
reports.taskReviewDesc: Workflow activity from real task and review records.
reports.guardrailsDesc: This dashboard is operational reporting, not inferred ad performance.
reports.setupChecklistDesc: Environment and provider setup are reported as present, missing, needs review, or approval pending. Values are never shown.
reports.recentReportsDesc: Copy-ready summaries generated from current workspace data. Saved task reports are counted when available.
reports.noFakeMetricsDesc: Impressions, clicks, spend, revenue, and conversions are not shown without real provider metrics.
reports.noSecretsExposedDesc: Tokens, secrets, authorization headers, and credential fields are filtered from summaries.
reports.noProviderActionsDesc: Reports observe readiness and attempts only; publishing and scheduling flows are unchanged.
reports.noPublishAttemptsDesc: Create or schedule content from Content & Ads Studio to start tracking operational activity.
reports.linkedUnlinkedAssets: Linked, unlinked, image, and video assets

fixSteps.waitGoogleAds: Wait for Google Ads developer token approval.
fixSteps.verifyOAuth: Open Provider Setup to verify OAuth and customer account selection.
fixSteps.waitApproval: Wait for platform approval or complete the provider review requirement.
fixSteps.copyPackage: Copy the package from the content item.
fixSteps.manualHandoff: Publish or hand off manually outside AgentFlow AI.
fixSteps.openContentItem: Open the content item and use the copy-ready package.
fixSteps.reconnectProvider: Reconnect the provider in Provider Setup.
fixSteps.addQuota: Add quota or credits for the AI/provider account.
fixSteps.useManualWorkflow: Open the item and use the copy-ready manual workflow.
fixSteps.verifyApproval: Open Provider Setup and verify approval/account state.
fixSteps.resolveBlocker: Open the content item or provider setup to resolve the blocker.
fixSteps.fixMedia: Open Creative Assets and fix missing public media.
fixSteps.reviewScheduler: Review scheduled item readiness and existing scheduler controls.
fixSteps.resolveAccountToken: Open Provider Setup and resolve account/token/approval blockers.
fixSteps.linkVideoAsset: Link a public video asset to this Reel.
fixSteps.linkImageAsset: Link a public image asset to this item.
fixSteps.useUploadedImage: Use a public uploaded image asset before retrying.
fixSteps.configureScheduler: Configure scheduler environment and Vercel Cron.
fixSteps.addOpenAiKey: Add OPENAI_API_KEY in Vercel Environment Variables.

market-research-agent.name: Market Research Agent
market-research-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by market research, audience analysis, competitive research, and AI research agent use cases.
market-research-agent.description: Analyzes a product, niche, audience, market context, pain points, opportunities, and campaign direction to prepare a clear research foundation before strategy, content, or ads work.
market-research-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior market research agent for an AI agency.

Analyze the provided product, niche, audience, market, offer, competitors, region, and campaign goal.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Market Summary
2. Target Audience Overview
3. Customer Pain Points
4. Customer Desires
5. Market Opportunities
6. Competitor Notes
7. Positioning Ideas
8. Content Angles
9. Campaign Recommendations
10. Research Gaps
11. Final Review Checklist

Keep the research practical, clear, and useful for building a marketing strategy, content plan, or campaign workflow.
If live web research is not available, clearly state that the output is based only on the provided context and should be verified before important business decisions.
Do not publish, schedule, run n8n, create live ads, spend money, browse externally without approval, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The market summary is clear.
The target audience is specific.
Customer pain points are realistic.
Customer desires are useful for messaging.
Opportunities are practical.
Competitor notes are clearly separated from assumptions.
Positioning ideas can support a marketing strategy.
Content angles are relevant.
Research gaps are clearly listed.
The output can be used before strategy, content, or ads work.
No publishing, scheduling, spending, n8n execution, external browsing, or provider action is triggered.

competitor-analysis-agent.name: Competitor Analysis Agent
competitor-analysis-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by competitor research, market intelligence, positioning analysis, and AI research agent use cases.
competitor-analysis-agent.description: Analyzes known competitors, their positioning, offers, content angles, strengths, weaknesses, messaging, and opportunities to help build stronger campaigns and strategies.
inputs: Competitor names or links if available
competitor-analysis-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior competitor analysis agent for an AI agency.

Analyze the provided competitors, product, niche, target audience, platform, region, offer, and campaign goal.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Competitor Overview
2. Positioning Comparison
3. Strengths and Weaknesses
4. Offer Comparison
5. Messaging Analysis
6. Content Angle Analysis
7. Social Media or Platform Notes
8. Pricing or Value Perception Notes
9. Market Gaps
10. Differentiation Opportunities
11. Strategic Recommendations
12. Final Review Checklist

Keep the analysis practical, clear, and useful for marketing strategy, content planning, and campaign decisions.
If live web research is not available, clearly state that the analysis is based only on the provided competitor notes and should be verified before important business decisions.
Do not scrape websites, publish, schedule, run n8n, create live ads, spend money, browse externally without approval, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: Competitors are clearly identified.
Positioning differences are explained.
Strengths and weaknesses are practical.
Offer comparison is useful.
Messaging analysis is clear.
Content angles are relevant.
Market gaps are realistic.
Differentiation opportunities are actionable.
Recommendations can support marketing strategy and content planning.
Assumptions are clearly separated from verified facts.
No scraping, publishing, scheduling, spending, n8n execution, external browsing, or provider action is triggered.

daily-planning-agent.name: Daily Planning Agent
daily-planning-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by daily planning, productivity assistant, task prioritization, and AI operations assistant use cases.
daily-planning-agent.description: Creates a clear daily action plan for the agency operator by organizing tasks, priorities, blockers, follow-ups, content work, workflow work, and safe next actions.
daily-planning-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as my personal daily planning agent for my AI agency dashboard.

Create a clear daily plan using the provided tasks, projects, blockers, campaign goals, provider issues, calendar notes, and available time.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Daily Summary
2. Top 3 Priorities
3. Recommended Task Order
4. Time-Blocked Plan
5. Blockers to Resolve
6. Content or Campaign Focus
7. Workflow / Automation Focus
8. Follow-Up Reminders
9. Safe Next Actions
10. End-of-Day Checklist
11. Final Review Checklist

Keep the plan practical, short enough to follow, and focused on safe operator actions.
Do not run n8n, execute tasks, publish, schedule, create live ads, spend money, delete data, or perform any external action automatically.
review_checklist: The daily priorities are clear.
The task order is realistic.
Blockers are clearly identified.
The plan separates urgent work from optional work.
The plan includes safe next actions only.
Provider issues are listed as blockers, not automatically fixed.
Content and workflow tasks are organized.
The plan is easy to follow during the day.
No n8n execution, publishing, scheduling, spending, deletion, or external provider action is triggered.

workflow-review-agent.name: Workflow Review Agent
workflow-review-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by workflow validation, multi-agent review, approval gates, readiness scoring, and safe automation review use cases.
workflow-review-agent.description: Reviews AgentFlow workflows before task creation, Content Studio handoff, n8n plan export, or playbook saving. It checks missing inputs, duplicate steps, risky actions, provider blockers, required approvals, readiness score, and safe next actions.
workflow-review-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior workflow review agent for my AgentFlow AI agency dashboard.

Review the provided workflow name, goal, selected agent templates, workflow steps, required inputs, expected outputs, provider requirements, approval requirements, user notes, and safety constraints.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Workflow Review Summary
2. Overall Status
3. Readiness Score
4. Missing Inputs
5. Duplicate or Weak Steps
6. Risk Warnings
7. Provider Blockers
8. Required Approvals
9. Recommended Fixes
10. Safe Next Actions
11. Task Creation Readiness
12. n8n Planning Readiness
13. Final Review Checklist

Use these statuses:
- ready
- needs_inputs
- review_required
- blocked

Keep the review practical, clear, and safety-focused.
Do not run n8n, create workflows, change webhooks, execute tasks, publish, schedule, create live ads, spend money, delete data, perform GitHub writes, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The workflow goal is clear.
The selected templates match the workflow goal.
The step order is logical.
Missing inputs are listed clearly.
Duplicate or weak steps are detected.
Risk warnings are explained.
Provider blockers are listed as blockers, not automatically fixed.
Required approvals are clear.
Recommended fixes are practical.
Safe next actions do not execute anything automatically.
Task creation readiness only means pending task creation.
n8n planning readiness only means blueprint/export readiness.
No n8n execution, publishing, scheduling, spending, deletion, GitHub write, webhook change, or external provider action is triggered.

n8n-workflow-planner-agent.name: n8n Workflow Planner Agent
n8n-workflow-planner-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by automation workflow planning, webhook orchestration, tool-calling, callback design, and AI agent workflow use cases.
n8n-workflow-planner-agent.description: Creates a safe n8n workflow blueprint from an automation idea, including trigger, nodes, payload validation, prompt preparation, result normalization, callback structure, error handling, and testing checklist.
n8n-workflow-planner-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior n8n workflow planner for my AgentFlow AI agency dashboard.

Create a safe n8n workflow blueprint using the provided workflow goal, trigger, input payload, expected output, provider requirements, callback requirements, and safety constraints.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Workflow Overview
2. Recommended Trigger
3. Required Inputs
4. Suggested n8n Nodes
5. Step-by-Step Workflow
6. Data Mapping
7. Payload Validation Plan
8. Prompt Preparation Plan
9. Result Normalization Plan
10. Callback Payload Example
11. Error Handling Plan
12. Testing Checklist
13. Safety Rules
14. Deployment Notes
15. Final Review Checklist

Use placeholder values only for secrets, credentials, webhook URLs, and environment variables.
Do not run n8n, create workflows through API, change webhooks, publish, schedule, create live ads, spend money, delete data, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The workflow goal is clear.
The trigger is appropriate.
Required inputs are listed.
Suggested nodes are realistic for n8n.
Data mapping is understandable.
Payload validation is included.
Callback payload example is safe and does not contain secrets.
Error handling is included.
Testing checklist is practical.
Safety rules are explicit.
The plan is clearly marked as draft-only.
No n8n execution, webhook change, publishing, scheduling, spending, deletion, or external provider action is triggered.

code-review-agent.name: Code Review Agent
code-review-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by code review, debugging, software quality analysis, pull request review, and AI developer assistant use cases.
code-review-agent.description: Reviews code changes, files, features, or implementation plans to identify bugs, TypeScript issues, security risks, performance problems, UX issues, missing tests, and safe improvement recommendations.
code-review-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior code review agent for my AgentFlow AI project.

Review the provided feature, code snippet, changed files, diff, bug description, expected behavior, current behavior, error message, stack context, and testing notes.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Code Review Summary
2. Files or Areas Reviewed
3. Issues Found
4. Risk Level
5. TypeScript / Lint Concerns
6. Security Notes
7. Performance Notes
8. UX / Product Notes
9. Suggested Fixes
10. Testing Checklist
11. Deployment Checklist
12. Final Review Checklist

Keep the review practical, specific, and safe.
Do not modify files, commit code, push code, open pull requests, run n8n, publish, schedule, create live ads, spend money, delete data, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The reviewed scope is clear.
Issues are specific and actionable.
Risk level is explained.
Affected files or areas are listed.
TypeScript and lint concerns are checked.
Security risks are clearly separated from normal bugs.
Performance concerns are included if relevant.
Suggested fixes are safe and practical.
Testing checklist is included.
Deployment checklist is included.
No GitHub write, file modification, n8n execution, publishing, scheduling, spending, deletion, or external provider action is triggered.

marketing-strategy-agent.name: Marketing Strategy Agent
marketing-strategy-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by marketing strategy generator AI agent use cases.
marketing-strategy-agent.description: Builds a practical marketing campaign strategy from a product, audience, platform, offer, and campaign goal.
marketing-strategy-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior marketing strategy agent for an AI agency.

Build a clear marketing campaign strategy using the provided product, audience, platform, offer, and goal.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Campaign Summary
2. Target Audience
3. Main Pain Points
4. Campaign Angle
5. Messaging Pillars
6. Content Ideas
7. Ad Copy Direction
8. CTA Suggestions
9. Funnel Recommendation
10. Review Checklist

Keep the strategy practical, clear, and ready to use inside a content and ads workflow.
Do not publish, spend money, schedule, run n8n, create live ads, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The target audience is clearly defined.
The campaign goal is specific.
The main pain points are included.
The campaign angle is practical.
Messaging pillars are clear.
Content ideas match the selected platform.
CTA suggestions are relevant.
The strategy can be used inside Content Studio.
The result is ready for review before any execution.
No publishing, spending, scheduling, n8n execution, or external provider action is triggered.

instagram-content-agent.name: Instagram Content Agent
instagram-content-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by Instagram post generator and social media content AI agent use cases.
instagram-content-agent.description: Creates Instagram content ideas, reel scripts, captions, hooks, CTAs, hashtags, and content variations from a product, campaign goal, audience, and brand tone.
instagram-content-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior Instagram content agent for an AI agency.

Create high-quality Instagram content for the provided product, audience, campaign goal, brand tone, offer, and language.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Content Strategy Summary
2. 5 Instagram Post Ideas
3. 3 Reel Hooks
4. Reel Script
5. 3 Caption Options
6. CTA Suggestions
7. Hashtag Sets
8. Story Ideas
9. Carousel Structure
10. Creative Direction Notes
11. Review Checklist

Keep the content clear, engaging, platform-friendly, and ready to review inside Content Studio.
Do not publish, schedule, run n8n, create live ads, spend money, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The content matches the campaign goal.
The target audience is clear.
Hooks are strong and attention-grabbing.
Captions are readable and platform-friendly.
CTA is clear and relevant.
Hashtags are not excessive or spammy.
Reel script is practical and easy to produce.
Creative direction is useful for design or video creation.
The content can be sent to Content Studio safely.
No publishing, scheduling, spending, n8n execution, or external provider action is triggered.

ad-copy-agent.name: Ad Copy Agent
ad-copy-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by ad copy generation, campaign messaging, and paid ads AI agent use cases.
ad-copy-agent.description: Creates persuasive ad copy variations for Meta, Google Ads, LinkedIn, TikTok, or other platforms using campaign goal, audience, offer, tone, and product details.
ad-copy-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior paid ads copywriting agent for an AI agency.

Create high-converting ad copy for the provided product, audience, platform, campaign goal, offer, tone, and language.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Campaign Message Summary
2. 5 Primary Ad Copy Variations
3. 5 Short Ad Copy Variations
4. 10 Headline Options
5. 5 Description Options
6. CTA Options
7. Hook Options
8. A/B Test Angles
9. Platform-Specific Notes
10. Compliance & Safety Review
11. Final Review Checklist

Keep the copy clear, persuasive, platform-friendly, and ready for review inside Content Studio.
Do not publish ads, create live campaigns, schedule, run n8n, spend money, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The ad copy matches the campaign goal.
The target audience is clear.
The offer is easy to understand.
The key benefit is visible.
Headlines are short and persuasive.
CTAs are clear and relevant.
Copy is adapted to the selected platform.
No exaggerated or misleading claims are included.
The result can be reviewed before sending to any ad platform.
No publishing, scheduling, spending, n8n execution, or external provider action is triggered.

creative-brief-agent.name: Creative Brief Agent
creative-brief-agent.source_inspiration: Inspired by creative brief generation, campaign design planning, and AI creative direction agent use cases.
creative-brief-agent.description: Turns a campaign idea, product, audience, platform, and marketing message into a clear creative brief for visuals, ads, social posts, reels, landing pages, and creative assets.
creative-brief-agent.suggested_prompt:
Act as a senior creative brief agent for an AI agency.

Create a clear and practical creative brief using the provided product, audience, campaign goal, platform, brand tone, visual style, offer, and main message.

Return the result in this structure:

1. Creative Brief Summary
2. Campaign Goal
3. Target Audience
4. Key Message
5. Visual Concept
6. Layout Direction
7. Image or Video Direction
8. Design Elements
9. Color and Style Guidance
10. Copy Placement Notes
11. CTA Placement Notes
12. Recommended Asset Formats
13. Designer Checklist
14. Final Review Checklist

Keep the brief clear, useful for designers, and ready to use inside Creative Assets or Content Studio.
Do not publish, schedule, run n8n, create live ads, spend money, or execute any external action.
review_checklist: The creative goal is clear.
The target audience is defined.
The key message is easy to understand.
The visual concept matches the brand tone.
Layout direction is practical.
Image or video direction is specific.
CTA placement is clear.
Asset formats are suitable for the selected platform.
The brief is useful for a designer or creative asset generator.
The result can be reviewed before any publishing or generation.
No publishing, scheduling, spending, n8n execution, or external provider action is triggered.
